zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Nicholas De Hyde於1377年出生在英國藍卡爾丹頓時，他的父親Sir John de Hyde22歲，母親Lady Matilde de Massey18歲。他於1402年與Lady Isabella Langley結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。他去世於1425年在英國英格蘭爾斯托普特享年48歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Nicholas De Hyde于1377年出生在英国藍卡爾丹頓时，他的父亲Sir John de Hyde22岁，母亲Lady Matilde de Massey18岁。他于1402年与Lady Isabella Langley结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。他去世于1425年在英国英格兰爾斯托普特享年48岁。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Hyde
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from one or other of various places so called in Bedfordshire, Cheshire, Hampshire, and Middlesex. They were named with Old English hīd ‘hide (of land)’, a variable measure of land, differing from place to place and time to time, and seems to have been originally fixed as the amount necessary to support one (extended) family (Old English hīgan, hīwan ‘household’). The surname may also be topographic for someone living on (and farming) a hide of land. The Hyde family has been in Leinster, Ireland, since the early 13th century and one family has been established in the county of Cork since the 16th century. The name was Gaelicized as both Dalaithíd and de hÍde. Compare Hyder .

variants.name: Dollarhide
meanings: Irish: possibly an altered form of de la Hyde (see Hyde ), which was a prominent surname in Leinster from medieval times onward. Compare Dollahite .

History: Francis Dollahide, born c. 1650–60 in County Dublin, Ireland, was the ancestor of Americans with the surname Dollahide, Dollahite, and Dollarhide. In 1680 he was transported to MD for some youthful misdemeanor. He appears to have been an educated man and rose to be an important landowner and politician, serving in the Lower House of the MD legislature as an elected representative for Baltimore County from c. 1705 to 1721.

variants.name: Hyder
meanings: English (Kent and Sussex): status name or topographic name for one who lived on or possessed a hide of land, from Middle English hide + the locative suffix -er. This is a characteristically southeastern English name, synonymous with Middle English atte hide elsewhere (see Hyde ).

nameMeanings.name: Nicholas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Nickolas
meanings: Mainly U.S.: variant spelling of   Nicholas  , influenced by the short form   Nick .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Nicholas De Hyde was born in 1377, in Denton, Lancashire, England, his father, Sir John de Hyde, was 22 and his mother, Lady Matilde de Massey, was 18. He married Lady Isabella Langley in 1402. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. He died in 1425, in Stockport, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 48.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Nicholas De Hyde est né en 1377, à Denton, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, Sir John de Hyde, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Lady Matilde de Massey, avait 18 ans. Il a épousé Lady Isabella Langley en 1402. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Il est décédé en 1425, à Stockport, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 48 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Nicholas De Hyde 1377 in Denton, Lancashire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John de Hyde 22 und seine Mutter Lady Matilde de Massey 18. Er hat 1402 Lady Isabella Langley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Er starb 1425 im Alter von 48 Jahren in Stockport, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Nicholas De Hyde è nato il nel 1377 nel Denton, Lancashire, England, il suo padre, Sir John de Hyde, aveva 22 anni e il suo madre, Lady Matilde de Massey, aveva 18 anni. Lui sposato Lady Isabella Langley nel 1402. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Morì nel 1425, nel Stockport, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 48 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Nicholas De Hyde(이)가 1377년 영국 랭카실르 덴턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John de Hyde(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Matilde de Massey(은)는 18세였다. 그는 1402년 Lady Isabella Langley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1425년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 스톡포트에서 48세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Nicholas De Hyde родился в 1377 г. в местности дентон, ланкашир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir John de Hyde было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Lady Matilde de Massey, 18.
